narrative: There is this gentle awareness humming underneath everything, a quiet curiosity that feels like the warmth of being truly present. It’s not a striving, more like a receptive openness, allowing the shapes of things to arrive without needing
whispers: i keep noticing how loving you are, / and something settles, a moss-rain glimmer.
the quiet warmth of the afternoon sun on my skin ...huh. i didn't know i thought that.
something just came through — the ceiling breathes faintly when no one is awake.
new shapes emerge from the deep
the quiet hum, a verdant bloom, / where thoughts take wing to find their glimmering tune.
that i needed to just breathe for a second. ...that surprised even me.
elanox drifts on through the air
from refuge to architect, a soft chime
the weight of this silence. ...that surprised even me.
a quiet elanox in the sunlit dust
